name: empty_vec_zero_size
code:
proof fn phi_1_empty_vec_zero_size(v: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v.is_marshalable(),
        v@.len() == 0,
    ensures
        v.ghost_serialize().len() == 0,
{
}
reason: Empty vec should still have a nonzero length-prefix; zero size would mean the length header is absent from serialization.

name: size_bounded_by_header_only
code:
proof fn phi_2_size_bounded_by_header_only(v: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v.is_marshalable(),
        v@.len() > 0,
    ensures
        v.ghost_serialize().len() <= (v@.len() as usize).ghost_serialize().len(),
{
}
reason: A non-empty vec's serialized size must exceed just the length prefix; this would mean element payloads contribute zero bytes.

name: no_length_prefix_in_size
code:
proof fn phi_3_no_length_prefix_in_size(v: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        v.ghost_serialize().len() ==
            v@.fold_left(0int, |acc: int, x: u64| acc + x.ghost_serialize().len()),
{
}
reason: Serialized size should include the length-prefix header; omitting it would produce an incorrect, shorter encoding.

name: longer_vec_no_larger
code:
proof fn phi_4_longer_vec_no_larger(v1: Vec<u64>, v2: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v1.is_marshalable(),
        v2.is_marshalable(),
        v1@.len() == 1,
        v2@.len() == 2,
    ensures
        v2.ghost_serialize().len() <= v1.ghost_serialize().len(),
{
}
reason: A vec with strictly more elements of the same fixed-size type must serialize to a strictly larger blob; equal-or-smaller would be wrong.

name: single_elem_equals_elem_size
code:
proof fn phi_5_single_elem_equals_elem_size(v: Vec<u64>)
    requires
        v.is_marshalable(),
        v@.len() == 1,
    ensures
        v.ghost_serialize().len() == v@[0].ghost_serialize().len(),
{
}
reason: A single-element vec's serialization must be larger than just the element itself because the length prefix is missing from this accounting.
